findings.summary: Spec test 3b only half-delivered: no test covers repair-exhaustion with a PARSEABLE failing (REWORK) final review; mutation 'adopt REWORK as passing' survives the full suite (84 passed). Add one test: final repair_review.md = 'VERDICT: REWORK' with no repair bullets -> assert synthetic BLOCKED verdict.json (source repair_exhausted, raw_review_path null). No production-code change required.
findings.evidence: grep -rn repair_exhausted tests/ matches only the new test file; its negative test uses an unparseable review which the shared parser maps to BLOCKED, so widening the adoption set to REWORK is invisible. Exposure bounded: execute_provider_phase re-gates 'verdict not in PASSING_VERDICTS -> block_provider_run', so the mutant cannot merge a non-passing phase.

findings.summary: parse_final_repair_review falls back to phase review.md when the final attempt dir is absent; that file is always reviewer-authored (repair loop mirrors every repair review into it), so non-reviewer text cannot be adopted.

Dropping the required_repairs guard is an equivalent mutant: parse_review_text (tools/frontier/verdict.py:221) structurally forces required_repairs=[] for passing verdicts and is the only verdict source on this path. Guard kept as defense-in-depth.

mutation_tests.disposition: proven equivalent mutant via verdict.py:221; documented F3

mutation_tests.mutation: remove mid-pipeline continuation block in ready_wave_phases
skip done_check gate (if False), REVIEWED jumps straight to commit

validation_counts.status_doctor: 4 ok / 1 WARN (no live run dir in worktree, expected), exit 0

rework_resolution.repair: Executor added test_repair_exhaustion_parseable_rework_final_review_stays_synthetic_blocked to tests/unit/frontier/test_ralph_driver_resume_completeness.py: final repair_review.md is a clean parseable 'VERDICT: REWORK' with no repair bullets; asserts run_provider_repair_loop returns BLOCKED, phase status stays REWORK (not REVIEWED), verdict.json has verdict BLOCKED / source repair_exhausted / raw_review_path null, and the REPAIR_EXHAUSTED event carries no source or verdict keys. Test-only delta: git diff names only tools/frontier/ralph_driver.py (production), whose diff sha256 still equals 391bdb4c710212fab100a93368f7d8857f2d31376f9e13bc240c84ff6f1b24aa, byte-identical to the reviewed diff; the only other working-tree path is the (untracked) test file gaining the one new test.

re_review_evidence.mutation_a_rerun: Re-applied mutant: fidelity path adoption set widened to PASSING_VERDICTS | {"REWORK"} at ralph_driver.py:4985. Result: 1 failed, 84 passed — exactly the new test failed with assert 'REWORK' == 'BLOCKED'. Mutation (a) is now KILLED.
re_review_evidence.restore_verified: Mutant reverted; git diff tools/frontier/ralph_driver.py | sha256sum == 391bdb4c710212fab100a93368f7d8857f2d31376f9e13bc240c84ff6f1b24aa; suite re-passed 85.
re_review_evidence.parser_path_confirmed: Under the mutant the adopted verdict was REWORK, proving parse_review_text parses the test's review to a clean REWORK with required_repairs == [] — the test exercises the parseable-REWORK path, not the unparseable fallback.

rework_resolution.remaining_warnings: Informational findings F2 (review.md fallback is reviewer-authored-only) and F3 (required_repairs guard is a proven-equivalent mutant kept as defense-in-depth) stand as documented; no repair required.
